title: Oozy Jammy Eggs
summary: While the humble egg seems simple to cook, getting the perfect jammy yolks and soft whites is an art. These eggs are made with meal-prep in mind, ready for you to chuck on toast, add to salads or eat straight from the fridge for a high-protein snack.

method.description: Fill a medium saucepan with water to and bring to a rolling boil.
Use a spoon to gently lower in the eggs into the boiling water. Don’t overcrowd the pan or lower the heat.
Set a timer for 6 mins if the eggs are room temp, or 6 mins 30 secs if they’re cold.
While they boil, fill a bowl with cold water and ice. Once the timer goes, transfer the eggs to the ice bath for 3 mins to stop them cooking further.

Peel if you’re eating them now – run them under cold water as you go to make it easier. 
If you’re storing them, keep unpeeled eggs in the shell for up to 7 days in the fridge. Peeled eggs should be stored submerged in cold water in a sealed container – they’ll stay fresh for up to 4 days.
To reheat, pop a cold egg in a bowl of warm water for 5 mins. The yolk stays oozy and delicious.

chefs.name: Finn Tonry
chefs.summary: Finn Tonry (@Finntonry) is a chef and recipe developer who’s all about making healthy food exciting. After years working in some of London’s best restaurants, he’s now focused on creating high-protein dishes that look great, taste amazing, and actually make you feel good.
